steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the two authors would disagree about. Find the central tension between Text 1 and Text 2.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'tropical species': Neither text focuses the disagreement on tropical species — this misplaces the comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — 'assuming temperate-species females don't sing': Text 1 treats a singing Cassin's finch as evidence of a male, implying females rarely sing in temperate zones. Text 2 states female birdsong is more common among temperate species than currently assumed. Direct conflict. ✓
  • Choice C — 'reversing the relationship': This reverses which author holds which position. ✗
  • Choice D — 'claim not made by either author': Introduces something neither text addresses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
